headline:
Track your lifts,
see your progress.
description:
Gymnio logs every set, weight and rep — so you always know how far you've come.
Clean, fast, private. No account required.

feature_detail.title:
Know when it's time
to push harder.
feature_detail.description: Gymnio tracks your last 5 sessions per exercise and alerts you when you're ready to increase the weight. No guesswork — just clear, data-driven suggestions.

feature_highlight.title:
Your entire gym history,
always in your pocket.
feature_highlight.description: Every workout entry is saved locally on your device. Browse your full history, spot personal bests, and track volume over time — all offline, all private.

showcase_title: Everything you need to train smarter.

feature_list.title: Exercise Tracking
feature_list.description:
Log weight, reps and date.
Every session, every set.

feature_list.title: Progress Charts
feature_list.description:
Weight, reps and volume.
Visualized over time.

feature_list.title: Progression Alerts
feature_list.description:
Know when to add weight.
Based on your real data.

feature_list.title: kg & lbs
feature_list.description:
Switch units anytime.
Stored data stays correct.

feature_list.title: Privacy First
feature_list.description:
No account, no cloud.
Your data stays on device.

articles.title: Add your first exercise
articles.description: Learn how to create an exercise and start tracking.
articles.intro: Getting started with Gymnio is simple. First, you create an exercise — then you can log sets, weights and reps for it.

sections.heading: Create an exercise
sections.content: Tap the + button in the tab bar at the bottom. A sheet appears where you can type the exercise name (e.g. "Bench Press"). Tap Add to save it.

sections.heading: Open the exercise
sections.content: After creating it, the exercise appears in the Exercises tab. Tap on it to open the detail view where you can log entries.

sections.heading: Log your first set
sections.content: Enter the weight and number of reps, optionally change the date, then tap Add Entry. Your set is saved instantly.

articles.title: Navigating the app
articles.description: Overview of the four main tabs in Gymnio.
articles.intro: Gymnio has four sections accessible from the tab bar at the bottom of the screen.

sections.heading: Home
sections.content: Your personal dashboard showing stats, progress charts, top exercises by volume, and a progression alert when you're ready to increase weight. You can customize which cards appear via the Customize button.

sections.heading: Exercises
sections.content: A list of all your exercises. Tap an exercise to see its full history, charts and stats. Tap and hold to delete an exercise, or use the ⋯ menu in the detail view.

sections.heading: History
sections.content: A chronological log of every entry you've ever logged, newest first. Tap the trash icon to delete an individual entry.

sections.heading: Plus (+)
sections.content: The + tab at the bottom right opens the "Add Exercise" sheet so you can quickly create a new exercise from anywhere in the app.

articles.title: Log a workout entry
articles.description: How to add weight, reps and date for an exercise.
articles.intro: Every entry in Gymnio represents one set or session for a specific exercise.

sections.heading: Open an exercise
sections.content: Go to the Exercises tab and tap the exercise you want to log for.

sections.heading: Enter weight and reps
sections.content: Fill in the weight field and the reps field at the top of the detail view. The weight is entered in your selected unit (kg or lbs).

sections.heading: Change the date
sections.content: By default the current date is selected. Tap the calendar icon to pick a different date — useful when logging a session from yesterday.

sections.heading: Save the entry
sections.content: Tap Add Entry. The entry appears immediately in the history list below and is counted in your stats.

articles.title: Delete a workout entry
articles.description: How to remove an entry from your history.
articles.intro: You can delete individual entries from both the exercise detail view and the history tab.

sections.heading: From exercise detail
sections.content: Scroll down to the history list in the exercise detail view. Tap the trash icon on the right side of any entry.

sections.heading: From History tab
sections.content: Open the History tab. Tap the trash icon next to the entry you want to remove.

sections.heading: Confirm deletion
sections.content: A confirmation alert will appear. Tap Delete to confirm. This action cannot be undone.

articles.title: Delete an exercise
articles.description: How to remove an exercise and all its entries.
articles.intro: Deleting an exercise also removes all logged entries for that exercise.

sections.heading: Open the exercise
sections.content: Tap on the exercise in the Exercises tab to open its detail view.

sections.heading: Use the menu
sections.content: Tap the ⋯ button in the top-right corner of the detail view, then select Delete Exercise.

sections.heading: Confirm
sections.content: Tap Delete in the confirmation alert. The exercise and all its entries are permanently removed.

articles.title: When do charts appear?
articles.description: Charts unlock after a minimum number of entries.
articles.intro: Gymnio needs at least 3 entries for an exercise before it can draw meaningful charts.

sections.heading: Locked chart state
sections.content: If you have fewer than 3 entries, you'll see a locked chart placeholder showing your current progress, e.g. "Charts unlock at 3 entries (1/3)".

sections.heading: Three chart types
sections.content: Once unlocked, you can swipe between three charts: Weight over time, Reps over time, and Volume (weight × reps) over time.

sections.heading: Home dashboard charts
sections.content: The home screen also shows a chart for your top exercises once you have at least 3 total entries logged.

articles.title: Progression alerts
articles.description: How Gymnio tells you when to increase the weight.
articles.intro: Gymnio automatically detects when you're ready to progress to a heavier weight.

sections.heading: How it works
sections.content: If your last 5 sessions for an exercise all have the same weight, Gymnio suggests increasing by 2.5 kg (or the equivalent in lbs). This appears as a card at the top of your home screen.

sections.heading: Customize visibility
sections.content: You can hide or show the progression alert card on your home screen via Customize Dashboard.

articles.title: Personal bests (PB)
articles.description: How Gymnio marks your heaviest lifts.
articles.intro: Gymnio automatically highlights your personal best (heaviest weight) for each exercise.

sections.heading: Trophy icon
sections.content: In the exercise history list, entries that match your maximum weight are marked with a trophy icon. There can be multiple PBs if you've matched your best weight in different sessions.

sections.heading: Summary card
sections.content: The summary card at the top of each exercise detail view shows your all-time max weight, max reps and total entry count.

articles.title: Switch between kg and lbs
articles.description: How to change your preferred weight unit.
articles.intro: Gymnio supports both kilograms (kg) and pounds (lbs). All stored data stays in kg internally — only the display converts.

sections.heading: Auto-detection
sections.content: On first launch, Gymnio reads your iOS measurement system preference from Settings → General → Language & Region → Measurement System. If it's set to US, lbs is used by default.

sections.heading: Change manually
sections.content: Open the Gymnio Settings tab (gear icon on the home screen). Under Preferences, tap Weight Unit and choose kg or lbs.

sections.heading: Existing data
sections.content: Switching units does not alter any stored data. All entries are always stored in kg and converted for display. Your history remains fully accurate.

articles.title: Change the app language
articles.intro: Gymnio is available in German and English.

sections.heading: Change language
sections.content: Go to Settings in Gymnio and tap Language. Select Deutsch or English. The app updates immediately.

sections.heading: Auto-detection
sections.content: By default Gymnio uses your device language. If your device is set to German, the app launches in German.

articles.title: Dark mode and appearance
articles.description: How to switch between light, dark, and system theme.
articles.intro: Gymnio supports Light Mode, Dark Mode, and follows your system setting.

sections.heading: Change theme
sections.content: Open Settings and tap Appearance. Choose between System (follows iOS), Light, or Dark.

articles.title: Customize the home dashboard
articles.description: Show or hide sections on your home screen.
articles.intro: You can control exactly which cards appear on your home dashboard.

sections.heading: Open customization
sections.content: On the Home tab, scroll down and tap Customize Dashboard. Alternatively, tap the slider icon.

sections.heading: Available toggles
sections.content: You can individually show or hide: Progression Alert, Stats (total workouts, volume, exercises), Charts, and Top Exercises.

sections.heading: Changes apply instantly
sections.content: Toggles take effect immediately — no save button needed.

articles.title: Where is my data stored?
articles.description: All workout data stays on your device.
articles.intro: Gymnio is built privacy-first. Your workout data never leaves your device.

sections.heading: Local only
sections.content: All exercises and entries are stored locally using iOS's built-in storage. No account, no server, no cloud sync.

sections.heading: No tracking
sections.content: Gymnio does not track your location, movements, or training patterns. No analytics data is sent to any server.

sections.heading: Open source
sections.content: Gymnio's source code is publicly available on GitHub. You can verify exactly what the app does with your data.

articles.title: What happens if I delete the app?
articles.description: Understand what data is removed when uninstalling.
articles.intro: Because all data is stored locally, deleting the app also deletes all your workout data.

sections.heading: No cloud backup
sections.content: Gymnio does not use iCloud or any cloud backup service. If you delete the app, your exercise history is permanently removed.

sections.heading: Before deleting
sections.content: If you plan to reinstall or switch devices, note that your data cannot be transferred automatically at this time. We are considering export features for a future update.

articles.title: Charts are not showing
articles.description: Why charts might not be visible yet.
articles.intro: If you don't see charts for an exercise, there is usually a simple reason.

sections.heading: Not enough entries
sections.content: Charts require at least 3 entries for the exercise. Until then, a locked placeholder is shown with your current progress (e.g. 2/3).

sections.heading: Home dashboard charts
sections.content: The home screen chart is hidden if you have fewer than 3 total entries across all exercises, or if you've turned off charts in Customize Dashboard.

articles.title: Progression alert not appearing
articles.description: Why the weight increase suggestion might not show.
articles.intro: The progression alert only appears under specific conditions.

sections.heading: Condition
sections.content: Gymnio triggers a progression suggestion when your last 5 entries for an exercise all have exactly the same weight. If your recent sessions have varied weights, no alert appears.

sections.heading: Hidden by settings
sections.content: Check that the Progression Alert is enabled in Customize Dashboard on the home screen.

articles.title: App crashes or freezes
articles.description: Steps to fix app stability issues.
articles.intro: If Gymnio is crashing or not responding, try these steps.

sections.heading: Force quit and restart
sections.content: Swipe up from the bottom of your screen and swipe away Gymnio. Reopen the app.

sections.heading: Update the app
sections.content: Make sure you have the latest version installed. Open the App Store and check for updates.

sections.heading: Contact support
sections.content: If the issue persists, please open a GitHub issue at github.com/alexmen656/gymapp with details about what happened.

hero.title: What's New?
hero.description: All updates, new features and improvements at a glance.

releases.title: Units, Charts & Polish
releases.description: This update brings kg/lbs support, improved charts that unlock earlier, a fully redesigned empty state experience, and lots of small details that make Gymnio feel even more at home on iOS 26.

features.title: kg & lbs support
features.description: Auto-detects your iOS measurement setting. Switch anytime in Settings.

features.title: Charts from 3 entries
features.description: Charts now unlock after just 3 entries instead of 5, with a progress indicator while locked.

features.title: Instant dashboard customization
features.description: Toggle home screen sections and changes apply immediately — no save button.

releases.title: Dashboard & Progression Alerts
releases.description: The home screen is now fully customizable. Gymnio also now detects when you've been lifting the same weight for 5 sessions in a row and suggests increasing the load.
changelog: Customizable home dashboard (show/hide stats, charts, top exercises)
Progression alert card when ready to increase weight

releases.title: Charts & Personal Bests
releases.description: Detailed progress charts per exercise and automatic personal best tracking.
changelog: Weight, reps and volume charts per exercise
Personal best (PB) trophy badge on entries
Summary card with max weight, max reps, total entries

releases.title: First Release
releases.description: The very first version of Gymnio. Simple, fast workout logging with a clean iOS 26 Liquid Glass design.
changelog: Log weight, reps and date per exercise

newsletter.title: Stay Updated
newsletter.description: Get notified about new Gymnio releases.

cta.title: Ready to train smarter?
cta.description: Download Gymnio for free on the App Store.

reviews.title: Finally a gym tracker that doesn't get in the way
reviews.text: I've tried so many workout apps and they're all bloated with features I don't need. Gymnio does exactly one thing perfectly — log my lifts and show me my progress. Love it.

reviews.name: Markus L.
texts: Started using Gymnio a month ago. Already hit 3 personal bests this week and the progression alert actually motivated me to push for a new max.

reviews.name: Sarah
reviews.text: I love that there's no account, no subscription, no nonsense. Open the app, log the set, done. The charts are a nice bonus to see how far I've come.

reviews.title: Exactly what I was looking for
reviews.text: Clean design, fast to use, and it just works offline. The kg/lbs toggle was the last thing I needed and now it's perfect.

reviews.name: Julia K.
reviews.text: The progression alert feature is underrated. It told me I was ready to go up in weight on squats before I even thought about it. Nailed it.

hero.title: Blog
hero.description: Training tips, app updates and insights from the Gymnio team.

featuredPost.title: Why tracking your lifts changes everything
featuredPost.excerpt: Most gym-goers train by feel. But the ones who make consistent progress are the ones who track their numbers. Here's why logging every set pays off — and how to make it a habit.

posts.title: New in v1.3: kg & lbs support
posts.excerpt: Gymnio now auto-detects your preferred unit from iOS settings and lets you switch anytime.

posts.title: How to use the progression alert
posts.excerpt: Gymnio tells you when you've been lifting the same weight for 5 sessions — here's what to do when it fires.

posts.title: Progressive overload: the only principle that matters
posts.excerpt: If you're not gradually increasing the demands on your muscles, you're not growing. Here's the science behind it.

posts.title: Understanding your progress charts
posts.excerpt: A breakdown of the three chart types in Gymnio and what to look for in each one.
